chief_complaint: A 15-year-old female patient presented with abdominal pain, fever, cough, and hallucinations.
history: The patient was initially considered to have an abdominal pathology. A preliminary diagnosis of meningitis was established after systematic clinical evaluations in the pediatric emergency department. The patient's cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) was obtained via lumbar puncture for diagnostic confirmation.
exam: Vital signs: not specified. Physical examination findings: not specified.
labs: Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) analysis: purulent-appearing CSF sample. Bio-Speedy(R) Meningitis/Encephalitis quantitative polymerase chain reaction (qPCR) MX-17 Panel (Bioeksen, Turkiye) multiplex PCR kit identified N.meningitidis as the causative pathogen. Real-time (RT)- PCR-based method identified the serogroup of N.meningitidis as serogroup C.
differentials: Meningitis due to other bacterial serogroups
correct_ranking: Meningitis due to other bacterial serogroups
